name: Ohio State University Extension - Cuyahoga County
description_short: Ohio State University Extension's Cuyahoga County office provides free educational programs and community development services to residents, including 4-H youth development, gardening education, and family consumer science programs.
description_long:
Ohio State University Extension - Cuyahoga County is a publicly funded educational outreach office operated by The Ohio State University College of Food, Agricultural, and Environmental Sciences (CFAES). Located in Cleveland, Ohio, it serves as a direct connection between university research and practical community education for Cuyahoga County residents.

The office offers a diverse range of free and low-cost educational programs focused on youth development, agricultural literacy, community gardening, family financial wellness, and environmental stewardship. Programs are delivered through workshops, training sessions, volunteer opportunities, and community partnerships. Notable programs include 4-H Youth Development for youth ages 5-18, the Dig In! community garden training initiative, Master Gardener Volunteer certification, and EFNEP (Expanded Food and Nutrition Education Program) nutrition education. The office previously administered SNAP-Ed programming until federal funding ended in September 2025.

What distinguishes this organization is its university affiliation, research-backed educational content, and completely free access to most programs. As a land-grant university extension office, it operates without requiring fees for participation in core programming. The office maintains a professional staff and volunteer network, offers in-person office services during business hours (Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM-4:30 PM), and actively engages the community through multiple social media channels and public events.

For consumers seeking financial or credit-related assistance, this is primarily an educational and community development resource rather than a direct financial services provider. While the organization emphasizes community wellness and likely touches on family financial topics through Family and Consumer Sciences programming, the website does not detail specific financial counseling, credit repair, debt management, or budgeting services. It functions as a community education hub rather than a consumer finance support organization.
pros: Completely free educational programs and services with no enrollment fees
University-backed content and programming rooted in peer-reviewed research
Direct local access through physical office in Cleveland with established phone and email contact
Multiple program offerings spanning youth development, nutrition, gardening, and community development
Active volunteer certification programs (Master Gardener) providing ongoing community engagement
Accessible hours Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-4:30 PM with multiple contact methods
Part of statewide land-grant extension network providing continuity and resource access
cons: Website does not clearly specify financial counseling, credit, or debt management services despite being categorized under free-help
Limited transparency on what Family and Consumer Sciences programming includes related to financial literacy
Recent loss of SNAP-Ed federal funding (ended September 2025) indicates reduced nutrition/financial support capacity
Website focus is primarily on youth development and gardening rather than consumer finance education
No mention of HUD-approved housing counseling, credit counseling certifications, or NFCC affiliation that would clarify consumer finance expertise
best_for: Families seeking free youth development and 4-H programming for children ages 5-18
Community members interested in starting or learning to maintain community gardens
Residents looking for evidence-based nutrition and food security education
diagnosis: Ohio State University Extension - Cuyahoga County is best for families and community members seeking free educational programs in youth development, gardening, nutrition, and community building rather than direct consumer financial services. The main caveat is that while categorized as a free-help organization, the website provides no clear evidence of financial counseling, credit education, debt management, or other consumer finance support services—making it unclear whether this categorization accurately reflects the organization's primary service offerings.
services: Dig In! community garden planning and design training
Master Gardener Volunteer certification and training
EFNEP (Expanded Food and Nutrition Education Program) nutrition education
Community garden support through Summer Sprout program
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
